title: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams

seo.title: Easy Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Italian Spaghetti alle Vongole with fresh Manila Clams. This easy recipe brings the taste of the Mediterranean to your kitchen. Perfect seafood pasta!

seo.h1: Authentic Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Spaghetti alle Vongole
Tips for the Best Clam Pasta
seo.imageAlt: A plate of Spaghetti alle Vongole with Manila clams, garlic, parsley, and a light white wine sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Whip up this classic Italian Spaghetti alle Vongole with sweet Manila clams for a taste of the sea at home.

description: Experience the true taste of the Italian coast with this exquisite Spaghetti alle Vongole featuring tender Manila clams. This classic dish combines al dente spaghetti with a vibrant white wine, garlic, and chili sauce, perfectly capturing the essence of Mediterranean flavors. It's a surprisingly simple yet elegant meal that will transport your senses straight to Italy.

ingredients.notes: like Pinot Grigio or Sauvignon Blanc

instructions.title: Prepare the Clams
instructions.text: Place scrubbed Manila clams in a large bowl with cold water and a tablespoon of salt for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours. This helps them purge any sand. Discard any clams that are open and do not close when tapped.

instructions.title: Cook the Spaghetti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package directions until al dente. Reserve about 1.5 cups of the pasta cooking water before draining.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large, deep skillet or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Sauté gently for 2-3 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and lightly golden, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Steam the Clams
instructions.text: Increase the heat to medium-high. Add the purged clams to the skillet, pour in the white wine, and cover immediately with a lid. Cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally, until all the clams have opened. Discard any clams that do not open.

instructions.title: Combine Pasta and Sauce
instructions.text: Add the drained spaghetti directly into the skillet with the opened clams and their liquid. Add half of the reserved pasta water and the chopped fresh parsley. Toss vigorously to combine, allowing the pasta to absorb the flavorful clam broth. If using, stir in the butter now for extra richness.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Continue to toss, adding more pasta water as needed, until the sauce emulsifies and coats the spaghetti beautifully. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Serve immediately in warm bowls, garnished with extra fresh parsley.

tips: Always purge your clams thoroughly to remove sand; this step is crucial for an enjoyable dish.
Don't overcook the spaghetti; it should be al dente as it will finish cooking in the clam sauce.
Reserve plenty of pasta water; it's essential for creating a silky, emulsified sauce that clings to the spaghetti.
article: Spaghetti alle Vongole Manila Clams is a beloved Italian Seafood dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
